IN.name: India
IN.summary: A vibrant land of color, culture, and endless contrasts. Home to 1.4 billion people and one of the world's fastest-growing economies.
IN.culture: From festivals to classical dance, India blends ancient heritage with modern energy. Experience Diwali celebrations, yoga practices, and Bollywood culture.
IN.temperature: Warm tropical climate in the south, cooler Himalayan air in the north. Summer 30-45°C, Winter 5-25°C.
IN.language: Hindi, English, and 21 other official languages

US.name: United States
US.summary: A vast country spanning ocean to ocean with diverse landscapes from coast to coast. The world's leading economy and technology hub.
US.culture: A melting pot of traditions, innovation, and iconic regional culture. Jazz heritage, Native American traditions, and Hollywood influence.
US.temperature: Highly varied. Alaska freezing (-50°C), Hawaii tropical (25-30°C), varies by region.
US.language: English (de facto), Spanish, Chinese, Vietnamese widely spoken
US.timezone: Multiple (EST, CST, MST, PST, AKST, HST)

JP.name: Japan
JP.summary: A refined blend of modern cities and serene historic temples. An island nation combining cutting-edge technology with ancient traditions.
JP.culture: Polished hospitality, seasonal rituals, and creative craftsmanship. Cherry blossoms, tea ceremonies, martial arts, and anime culture.
JP.temperature: Mild springs and autumns, humid summers (25-32°C), crisp winters (0-10°C) in most regions.

FR.name: France
FR.summary: A country celebrated for art, cuisine, and timeless landscapes. The cultural heart of Europe with world-class museums and vineyards.
FR.culture: Café moments, fashion, wine regions, and an elegant sense of living. Renaissance art, haute couture, and culinary excellence.
FR.temperature: Temperate climate with warm summers (20-25°C) and mild winters (0-8°C) in most regions.

GB.name: United Kingdom
GB.summary: An island nation of heritage and modernity. Home to Big Ben, royal palaces, and one of the world's most influential financial centers.
GB.culture: Historic castles, afternoon tea, football passion, and literary traditions. Shakespeare heritage, royal ceremonies, and rock music origins.
GB.temperature: Temperate oceanic with mild winters (2-8°C) and cool summers (14-20°C). Frequent rain throughout the year.

DE.name: Germany
DE.summary: The industrial powerhouse of Europe. Known for engineering excellence, beer culture, and a rich history spanning centuries.
DE.culture: Precision engineering, Oktoberfest celebrations, classical music heritage, and vibrant arts scene. Kraftwerk, Bauhaus, and Rammstein influence.
DE.temperature: Temperate continental with cold winters (-1 to 3°C) and warm summers (18-24°C). Moderate precipitation.

CA.name: Canada
CA.summary: A vast nation of natural wonders spanning six time zones. The world's second-largest country by land area with diverse ecosystems.
CA.culture: Multiculturalism, outdoor adventures, hockey passion, and indigenous heritage. Maple syrup culture, Niagara Falls, and Rocky Mountain traditions.
CA.temperature: Extreme variations. Arctic regions extremely cold (-40°C+), moderate (5-20°C) in populated areas, coastal regions milder.
CA.timezone: Multiple (EST, CST, MST, PST, AKST, NST)

AU.name: Australia
AU.summary: A unique continent-country of vast deserts, tropical rainforests, and pristine beaches. Home to the Great Barrier Reef and iconic wildlife.
AU.culture: Aboriginal heritage, surfing culture, laid-back lifestyle, and pioneering spirit. Boomerangs, aboriginal art, and outback traditions.
AU.temperature: Tropical in the north (24-32°C year-round), temperate in the south (10-25°C). Seasons reversed from Northern Hemisphere.
AU.timezone: Multiple (AWST, ACST, AEST, UTC+5:30 to UTC+10)

BR.name: Brazil
BR.summary: The largest country in South America and home to the Amazon Rainforest. A vibrant culture of music, sports, and natural diversity.
BR.culture: Samba dance, carnival celebrations, football passion, and Amazon indigenous cultures. Bossa nova music, capoeira martial arts, and beach culture.
BR.temperature: Tropical climate with high humidity. North tropical (25-32°C), South subtropical (15-28°C). High rainfall.

MX.name: Mexico
MX.summary: A land of ancient civilizations and vibrant colors. Rich in Aztec heritage, colonial architecture, and diverse natural landscapes.
MX.culture: Day of the Dead celebrations, indigenous traditions, muralism, and culinary excellence. Lucha Libre wrestling, folk dances, and pre-Columbian heritage.
MX.temperature: Highly varied. Highland areas 10-25°C, coastal areas 25-32°C. Rainy season May-October.

IT.name: Italy
IT.summary: The cradle of Western civilization and Renaissance. An iconic destination of art, history, fashion, and culinary traditions.
IT.culture: Renaissance masters, opera heritage, fashion capital Milan, and dolce vita lifestyle. Michelangelo, Leonardo da Vinci, and Italian design influence.
IT.temperature: Mediterranean climate. Northern regions 0-24°C, southern regions 10-28°C. Mild winters, hot summers.

ES.name: Spain
ES.summary: A passionate country of flamenco, bullfighting, and Mediterranean beauty. Vibrant culture with strong regional identities.
ES.culture: Flamenco dance, siesta tradition, bullfighting heritage, and artistic movements. Gaudí architecture, Salvador Dalí surrealism, and paella culture.
ES.temperature: Mediterranean climate. Coastal areas 15-28°C, inland regions 5-32°C. Dry summers, mild winters.

KR.name: South Korea
KR.summary: A dynamic nation blending high-tech innovation with deep traditions. From K-pop to ancient temples, South Korea leads the digital age.
KR.culture: K-pop music, traditional dance, gaming culture, and technological innovation. Taekwondo martial arts, tea ceremonies, and film industry excellence.
KR.temperature: Temperate continental. Winters very cold (-5 to 5°C), summers hot and humid (23-29°C). Clear autumn and spring.

SG.name: Singapore
SG.summary: A ultra-modern city-state and global financial hub. A blend of cultures, innovation, and pristine urban planning.
SG.culture: Multicultural harmony, business excellence, and culinary fusion. Chinese temples, Indian traditions, Malay heritage, and colonial architecture.
SG.temperature: Tropical climate, hot and humid year-round (24-32°C). High rainfall, especially November-January.

AE.name: United Arab Emirates
AE.summary: A desert nation transformed into a modern marvel. Home to the world's tallest building and a cosmopolitan cultural hub.
AE.culture: Islamic traditions blended with modern luxury, desert heritage, and international business culture. Falconry, pearl diving heritage, and hospitality.
AE.temperature: Desert climate, extremely hot in summer (40-50°C), mild in winter (15-25°C). Very dry with minimal rainfall.

EG.name: Egypt
EG.summary: An ancient civilization along the Nile River. Home to the pyramids and one of the world's oldest continuous cultures.
EG.culture: Ancient pharaonic heritage, Islamic traditions, and vibrant street life. Hieroglyphics, tomb treasures, and Nile River culture.
EG.temperature: Desert climate, extremely hot (30-45°C in summer), warm in winter (10-25°C). Cairo has moderate seasons.

ZA.name: South Africa
ZA.summary: The 'Rainbow Nation' of Africa. A diverse country with world-class natural beauty, wildlife, and vibrant multicultural society.
ZA.culture: Nelson Mandela legacy, ubuntu philosophy, tribal traditions, and jazz heritage. Apartheid history, tribal languages, and wine culture.
ZA.temperature: Temperate to subtropical. Summer (Oct-Mar) 25-35°C, Winter (Apr-Sep) 10-20°C. Water temperatures 15-23°C.
ZA.language: Zulu, Xhosa, Afrikaans, English, and 8 other official languages

AR.name: Argentina
AR.summary: A European-flavored South American nation. Known for tango, gaucho culture, wine, and vast expanses of pampas.
AR.culture: Tango dance passion, gaucho traditions, football obsession, and European-style café culture. Literary heritage and passionate people.
AR.temperature: Subtropical north (20-30°C), temperate center (12-25°C), cool south/Patagonia (5-15°C). Mild seasons.

CL.name: Chile
CL.summary: A narrow ribbon of land stretching along the Pacific coast. Diverse from Atacama Desert to Patagonian glaciers.
CL.culture: Indigenous Mapuche heritage, literary Nobel prizes, wine excellence, and mountain culture. Poetry, folk music, and desert traditions.
CL.temperature: Varies dramatically: Desert north (15-32°C), central Chile (8-28°C), Patagonia south (0-15°C) with rain.

TH.name: Thailand
TH.summary: The Land of Smiles. A Southeast Asian nation of ornate temples, stunning beaches, and warm hospitality.
TH.culture: Buddhist reverence, muay thai fighting, royal devotion, and spiritual practices. Monk traditions, spirit houses, and meditation culture.
TH.temperature: Tropical monsoon. Hot season (Mar-May) 32-40°C, rainy season (Jun-Oct) 25-32°C with humidity, cool season (Nov-Feb) 20-28°C.

CN.name: China
CN.summary: The world's most populous nation and second-largest economy. An ancient civilization with 5,000 years of history, now a global technology superpower.
CN.culture: Confucian values, tea culture, calligraphy, and martial arts. Dragon festivals, Lunar New Year, and a rich tradition of silk, porcelain, and poetry.
CN.temperature: Varied: tropical south (20-35°C), frigid northeast (-30°C winters), temperate central (5-25°C).
CN.language: Mandarin Chinese (Putonghua), Cantonese, and many regional dialects

RU.name: Russia
RU.summary: The world's largest country by land area, spanning 11 time zones. A nation of extraordinary cultural depth, vast wilderness, and imperial history.
RU.culture: Ballet, classical music, and literature define Russian culture. Pushkin, Tolstoy, Tchaikovsky, and Fabergé eggs represent centuries of artistic excellence.
RU.temperature: Extremely cold winters (-40°C in Siberia), warm summers (15-25°C) in western regions. Permafrost covers much of the north.

ID.name: Indonesia
ID.summary: An archipelago of 17,000+ islands and the world's largest Muslim-majority nation. Rich in biodiversity, ancient temples, and volcanic landscapes.
ID.culture: Batik textiles, wayang puppet theatre, gamelan music, and Hindu-Buddhist temple traditions. Bali's spiritual culture is world-renowned.
ID.temperature: Tropical and humid year-round (25-32°C). Wet season November-April, dry season May-October.

SA.name: Saudi Arabia
SA.summary: The birthplace of Islam and guardian of Mecca and Medina. A rapidly modernizing kingdom with vast oil wealth and ancient desert heritage.
SA.culture: Islamic traditions, Bedouin heritage, coffee culture, and falconry. Vision 2030 is transforming the kingdom into a global entertainment and tourism hub.
SA.temperature: Extremely hot desert climate (40-50°C in summer), mild winters (10-20°C). Coastal areas more humid.

TR.name: Turkey
TR.summary: A transcontinental nation bridging Europe and Asia. The heir to the Ottoman Empire, with breathtaking landscapes from Cappadocia to the Bosphorus.
TR.culture: Ottoman heritage, Sufi traditions, hammam culture, and carpet weaving. Istanbul's bazaars, whirling dervishes, and folk music define Turkish identity.
TR.temperature: Mediterranean coast (15-35°C), continental interior with cold winters (-10°C) and hot summers (30°C).

NG.name: Nigeria
NG.summary: Africa's largest economy and most populous nation. A powerhouse of culture, music, and entrepreneurship — known as the 'Giant of Africa'.
NG.culture: Afrobeats music, Nollywood film industry, vibrant festivals, and 250+ ethnic groups. Yoruba, Igbo, and Hausa traditions shape national identity.
NG.temperature: Tropical: south humid (25-32°C year-round), north semi-arid (hot days, cold nights). Rainy season April-October.

PK.name: Pakistan
PK.summary: A land of ancient civilizations and dramatic mountain landscapes. Home to the world's second-highest peak K2 and the ancient city of Mohenjo-daro.
PK.culture: Sufi music, truck art, polo in the northern mountains, and rich Mughal heritage. Hospitality (mehman-nawazi) is a cultural cornerstone.
PK.temperature: Varies widely: plains 25-45°C in summer, mountain regions below 0°C in winter. Monsoon season July-September.

PL.name: Poland
PL.summary: A resilient nation at the heart of Europe with a rich history of culture, science, and struggle. One of Europe's fastest-growing economies.
PL.culture: Chopin's piano music, Nobel laureates, amber jewelry, and vibrant folk traditions. Warsaw's reconstruction after WWII is a symbol of national spirit.
PL.temperature: Temperate continental: cold winters (-5 to 2°C), warm summers (18-25°C). Moderate rainfall year-round.

NL.name: Netherlands
NL.summary: A flat, tulip-filled nation below sea level, famous for windmills, cycling, and world-class art. The world's largest flower exporter.
NL.culture: Golden Age painting (Rembrandt, Vermeer), liberal values, cycling culture, and cheese markets. Dutch design and architecture are globally influential.
NL.temperature: Temperate oceanic: mild winters (0-7°C), cool summers (15-22°C). Frequent rain and wind.

SE.name: Sweden
SE.summary: A Scandinavian nation of forests, fjords, and midnight sun. A global leader in innovation, sustainability, and quality of life.
SE.culture: ABBA, IKEA, and Volvo originate here. Midsommar celebrations, fika coffee culture, and a deep connection with nature define Swedish life.
SE.temperature: Subarctic in the north (-20°C winters), temperate in the south (0-25°C). Long summer days, dark winters.

MA.name: Morocco
MA.summary: A gateway between Europe and Africa, where Sahara dunes meet Atlantic coasts. A land of medinas, riads, and ancient trade routes.
MA.culture: Berber traditions, Arabic calligraphy, hammam rituals, and souk markets. Fez's medieval medina is a UNESCO World Heritage Site.
MA.temperature: Mediterranean coast (15-28°C), Sahara desert extreme heat (40°C+), Atlas Mountains cooler (5-20°C).

PH.name: Philippines
PH.summary: An archipelago of 7,641 islands in Southeast Asia. Known for stunning beaches, warm people, and a unique blend of Malay, Spanish, and American influences.
PH.culture: Fiesta culture, jeepney art, religious devotion, and bayanihan community spirit. OFW culture and world-class hospitality define Filipino identity.
PH.temperature: Tropical: hot and humid year-round (25-32°C). Typhoon season June-October. Dry season December-May.

VN.name: Vietnam
VN.summary: An S-shaped nation of limestone karsts, rice paddies, and ancient towns. A rising economic star with extraordinary natural and cultural diversity.
VN.culture: Water puppet theatre, ao dai fashion, ancestor worship, and lantern festivals. Hội An's ancient town preserves centuries of trading history.
VN.temperature: Tropical south (25-35°C year-round), four seasons in the north (15-35°C). Monsoon May-November.

CO.name: Colombia
CO.summary: A land of eternal spring, salsa music, and stunning biodiversity. The only South American country with coastlines on both the Pacific and Atlantic.
CO.culture: Gabriel García Márquez's magical realism, Carnaval de Barranquilla, cumbia music, and coffee culture. Bogotá's Gold Museum holds 55,000 pre-Colombian artifacts.
CO.temperature: Varies by altitude: coastal lowlands (28-32°C), Bogotá highlands (7-19°C), Amazonian jungle (25-30°C).

GR.name: Greece
GR.summary: The cradle of Western civilization, democracy, philosophy, and the Olympic Games. A Mediterranean paradise of ancient ruins and island sunsets.
GR.culture: Ancient philosophy (Socrates, Plato, Aristotle), Orthodox Christian traditions, and the blue-domed churches of Santorini are globally iconic.
GR.temperature: Mediterranean: hot dry summers (25-38°C), mild winters (5-15°C). Islands enjoy 300+ days of sunshine.

PT.name: Portugal
PT.summary: A seafaring nation that once ruled the world's oceans. A melancholic, beautiful country of fado music, azulejo tiles, and Atlantic coastlines.
PT.culture: Fado music (UNESCO heritage), Age of Discoveries legacy, cork industry, and port wine culture. Lisbon's tram 28 and Sintra's palaces are iconic.
PT.temperature: Mediterranean: warm summers (22-30°C), mild winters (8-15°C). Atlantic coast cooler and windier.

NZ.name: New Zealand
NZ.summary: A remote Pacific nation of dramatic fjords, volcanic peaks, and Maori culture. The filming location of Middle-earth from Lord of the Rings.
NZ.culture: Maori haka, rugby passion, outdoor adventure culture, and sheep farming heritage. Bungee jumping was invented here.
NZ.temperature: Temperate oceanic: mild summers (18-25°C), cool winters (5-12°C). South Island colder. Frequent rainfall.

KE.name: Kenya
KE.summary: East Africa's jewel — home to the Great Migration, Maasai warriors, and snow-capped Mount Kenya. A hub of tech innovation on the continent.
KE.culture: Maasai beadwork, Swahili coastal culture, long-distance running champions, and vibrant Nairobi art scene. Harambee spirit of unity.
KE.temperature: Tropical: Nairobi Highland 15-26°C, coastal areas 25-32°C. Two rainy seasons (March-May, October-December).

IR.name: Iran
IR.summary: The ancient Persian Empire reborn. One of the world's oldest civilizations with magnificent architecture, poetry, and carpet-making traditions.
IR.culture: Persian poetry (Hafez, Rumi), Nowruz New Year, miniature painting, and bazaar culture. Hospitality (ta'arof) is a refined social art.
IR.temperature: Highly varied: arid desert (40°C+ summers, near-freezing winters), northern mountains with heavy snow, mild Caspian coast.

MY.name: Malaysia
MY.summary: A multicultural Southeast Asian nation where Malay, Chinese, and Indian cultures coexist harmoniously. Home to the iconic Petronas Twin Towers.
MY.culture: Batik cloth, wayang kulit shadow puppetry, and vibrant street art. Hari Raya, Chinese New Year, and Deepavali are all national holidays.
MY.temperature: Tropical: hot and humid year-round (25-32°C). Heavy rainfall, especially on the east coast October-February.
